name: Sendero del Pino Canaleja (SLC-VA 64) — circular desde La Parrilla
region_intro: Hiking around La Parrilla offers a variety of routes through the region's diverse landscape. The area features river paths along the Duero, as well as trails winding through valleys and wooded areas. Hikers can explore a mix of open terrain and shaded sections, with gentle elevation changes. The trails provide opportunities to experience the natural environment of La Parrilla. Best hiking trails around La Parrilla The most popular hiking route is Tudela de Duero — circular por la Senda del Duero (GR14), a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Senda del Duero, offering riverside views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Atalaya Trail, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This trail features a mix of open terrain and gentle climbs, providing views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Sendero del Valcorba (PRC-VA 21) — circular Santibáñez de Valcorba, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through the Valcorba valley, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around La Parrilla is defined by river paths, valleys, and wooded sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 7 easy routes and 19 moderate routes. The routes in La Parrilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore La Parrilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Parrilla?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes around La Parrilla, offering a variety of experiences. This includes 7 easy routes and 19 moderate routes, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near La Parrilla?
A: Hiking around La Parrilla features diverse terrain, including river paths along the Duero, trails winding through valleys, and wooded sections. You'll find a mix of open areas and shaded paths, generally with gentle elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in La Parrilla?
A: Yes, La Parrilla offers 7 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077527970/atalaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.3 miles (5.3 km) long and features gentle climbs with views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes around La Parrilla?
A: While there are no routes officially classified as 'difficult', La Parrilla has 19 moderate routes that offer a good workout. These trails often involve longer distances or more varied terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085787975/tudela-de-duero-circular-por-la-senda-del-duero-gr14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tudela de Duero — circular por la Senda del Duero (GR14)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.1 miles (14.6 km).

Q: Are there any circular walking routes in La Parrilla?
A: Yes, many routes in La Parrilla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077535602/sendero-del-valcorba-prc-va-21-circular-santibanez-de-valcorba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Valcorba (PRC®-VA 21)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5.2-mile (8.3 km) circular trail through the Valcorba valley.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks to see on hikes near La Parrilla?
A: While hiking, you can discover several points of interest. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4011321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hermitage of Traspinedo&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also offer views of the Duero River and the surrounding valleys.

Q: Are the hiking trails around La Parrilla suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy routes with gentle terrain that are suitable for families. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077524968/sendero-de-negredo-prc-va-32-circular-desde-traspinedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de Negredo (PRC-VA 32)&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short and have minimal elevation gain, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Parrilla?
A: The routes in La Parrilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from riverside paths to wooded sections, and the opportunities to experience the natural environment.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in La Parrilla?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many villages and starting points for popular routes, such as those near Tudela de Duero or Santibáñez de Valcorba, typically have designated parking areas or roadside spots. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: Are there public transport options to reach hiking trails in La Parrilla?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in La Parrilla can be limited, as is common in rural areas. Local bus services connect some of the larger towns, but reaching the exact starting point of a hike often requires a car. Planning your route in advance and checking local bus schedules is recommended.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Parrilla?
A: The best time for hiking in La Parrilla is generally during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are mild and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in open areas, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for hiking, particularly on lower elevation trails.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options in La Parrilla?
A: To find less crowded options, consider exploring some of the moderate routes that venture further into the valleys and wooded areas away from the more popular riverside paths. Early mornings or weekdays can also offer a more solitary experience on any trail.
tips: Finally we find the stone pine that gives its name to this route, the Canaleja pine, also known as "Pino Gordo".
